title: rosa × collina subsp. corymbifera
intro: Rosa × collina subsp. corymbifera, commonly known as the alpine wild rose, is a subspecies of rose that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its health benefits such as soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation. Rosa × collina subsp. corymbifera can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
